description: APT28 is a highly sophisticated Russian GRU-linked group known for election interference, military espionage, and credential harvesting operations against NATO member states.

techniques.notes: Primary initial access vector; uses weaponized Office documents with macro payloads.
Uses harvested credentials from phishing to blend in with legitimate user activity.
Uses Mimikatz and custom tools to dump LSASS memory for lateral movement credentials.
Uses HTTPS-based C2 to blend with legitimate web traffic; seen with X-Tunnel.
Profiles targets via LinkedIn and Twitter before spearphishing operations.
Encrypts payloads and uses custom packers to evade AV/EDR detection.
Uses X-Tunnel to exfiltrate data collected by X-Agent over encrypted channels.

source: MITRE ATT&CK G0007 / CISA / NSA Joint Advisory

description: APT29 is a Russian SVR-linked group known for long-duration, stealthy espionage campaigns. Masterminds of the SolarWinds supply chain attack — one of the most sophisticated operations ever documented.

techniques.notes: Hallmark technique; trojanized SolarWinds Orion update affected 18,000+ organizations.
Mimics legitimate processes and services to avoid detection during long dwell periods.
Searches for credentials stored in config files, scripts, and application data.
Abuses OAuth tokens and SAML assertions to move laterally across cloud environments.
Adds additional credentials to accounts or modifies permissions to maintain access.
Exploited CVE-2024-3400 in PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN as zero-day for initial access.
Automates collection and exfiltration of targeted data types at scale.

source: MITRE ATT&CK G0016 / CISA AA20-352A / Mandiant UNC2452

description: APT41 is a uniquely dual-purpose Chinese state-sponsored group conducting both espionage on behalf of the Chinese government and financially motivated cybercrime simultaneously — making them one of the most prolific and dangerous APTs.

techniques.notes: Rapidly weaponizes newly disclosed CVEs within hours of public disclosure.
Has trojanized software installers and gaming platforms to distribute malware.
Uses PowerShell for post-exploitation, payload delivery, and lateral movement.
Performs internal and external network scanning to map targets before exploitation.
Uses multi-hop proxies and compromised third-party infrastructure to mask C2.
Creates backdoor accounts on compromised systems for persistent access.

source: MITRE ATT&CK G0096 / DOJ Indictment 2020 / Mandiant

description: Lazarus Group is a North Korean state-sponsored group operating under the RGB (Reconnaissance General Bureau). Their primary mission is generating hard currency for the DPRK regime through financial theft, particularly cryptocurrency, while also conducting espionage and destructive attacks.

techniques.notes: Sends fake job offers and LinkedIn messages with malicious links or attachments to crypto/finance employees.
Entices targets to open malicious documents disguised as salary negotiation PDFs or job descriptions.
Injects BLINDINGCAN and custom RAT payloads into legitimate processes.
Deploys destructive wipers and ransomware (e.g., WannaCry) as cover or sabotage.
Modifies network device firmware to maintain persistence in high-value environments.
Primary mission outcome: SWIFT fraud, crypto exchange compromise, and DeFi protocol exploitation.

source: MITRE ATT&CK G0032 / UN Panel of Experts / US-CERT

description: LockBit is the world's most prolific Ransomware-as-a-Service operation. It provides affiliates with ransomware tooling and infrastructure in exchange for a percentage of ransom payments. Responsible for more ransomware attacks than any other group in 2022-2023.

techniques.notes: Affiliates purchase or use stolen credentials to authenticate to RDP, VPN, and web portals.
Core ransomware payload encrypts all accessible files and drops ransom note.
Deletes shadow copies and disables backup services before encryption to prevent recovery.
Identifies high-value data directories (finance records, PII, IP) for prioritized encryption and exfiltration.
Uses compromised credentials for RDP lateral movement across victim environments.
Exfiltrates data to cloud storage before encryption for double-extortion leverage.

description: Cl0p specializes in mass exploitation of zero-day vulnerabilities in widely used enterprise file transfer software, enabling them to breach hundreds of organizations simultaneously in single campaigns.

techniques.notes: Signature technique: mass exploitation of zero-days in MOVEit, GoAnywhere, and Accellion FTA.
Executes post-exploitation commands and scripts via cmd.exe on compromised servers.
Mass-collects data from file transfer servers before ransomware deployment or extortion.
Extorts organizations with threat of publishing stolen data on their leak site.
Deploys Cl0p ransomware for encryption in cases where extortion alone is insufficient.

description: KillNet is a pro-Russian hacktivist collective that emerged in 2022 following Russia's invasion of Ukraine. Primarily conducts DDoS campaigns against NATO countries, government infrastructure, and media outlets. Lower sophistication than APT groups but capable of sustained disruption.

techniques.notes: Primary weapon; conducts volumetric DDoS attacks using rented botnets and volunteer tools.
HTTP flood and UDP flood attacks targeting government web portals and hospital systems.
Recruits volunteer attackers via Telegram channels and coordinates attack timing publicly.
Uses Telegram and social media for target selection, recruitment, and propaganda.
